buttons.summary: Primary, secondary, tertiary, outlined, and text actions.
buttons.use_when: A user must trigger an explicit action.
buttons.avoid_when: The control is actually a navigation destination or passive label.
accessibility_notes: Preserve touch target size
Use visible labels for critical actions

platform_notes.compose: Use Button, FilledTonalButton, OutlinedButton, or TextButton from Material 3.
platform_notes.flutter: Use ElevatedButton, FilledButton, FilledButton.tonal, OutlinedButton, or TextButton with Material 3 theme.
platform_notes.web: Map to semantic role tokens; avoid non-MD3 custom variants.

icon-buttons.summary: Compact icon actions, including toggleable variants.
icon-buttons.use_when: A concise icon action is sufficient and discoverable.
icon-buttons.avoid_when: The action is critical and unlabeled or the icon is ambiguous.
accessibility_notes: Provide accessible labels
Use toggle semantics for toggled state

platform_notes.compose: Use standard, filled, filled tonal, or outlined icon button variants as appropriate.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm variant parity and selected-state theming.
platform_notes.web: Use clear labels and tokenized state feedback.

text-fields.summary: Outlined or filled MD3 text input fields.
text-fields.use_when: A user must input or edit structured text.
text-fields.avoid_when: The task needs freeform document editing or a picker is more appropriate.
accessibility_notes: Persistent label
Error and helper text must remain readable

platform_notes.compose: Use Material 3 text field APIs and keep labels/error states tokenized.
platform_notes.flutter: Use InputDecoration with Material 3 theme mapping.
platform_notes.web: Mirror label and stroke behavior with semantic variables.

cards.summary: Elevated, filled, or outlined grouping surface.
cards.use_when: Related information needs a shared surfaced container.
cards.avoid_when: Every list row is being cardified without hierarchy.
accessibility_notes: Preserve clear click target if interactive

platform_notes.compose: Prefer FilledCard, ElevatedCard, or OutlinedCard semantics.
platform_notes.flutter: Align card shape and tonal surfaces through theme tokens.
platform_notes.web: Use semantic surface roles before custom shadows.

chips.summary: Assist, filter, input, or suggestion chips.
chips.use_when: A light-weight selection, suggestion, or contextual action is needed.
chips.avoid_when: Primary navigation or high-emphasis primary action is required.
accessibility_notes: Ensure removable chips announce removal affordance

platform_notes.compose: Use chip family components with clear type selection.
platform_notes.flutter: Map chip states carefully; variant APIs may differ.
platform_notes.web: Keep them tokenized and do not repurpose as tabs by default.

tabs.summary: Peer content switching within one context.
tabs.use_when: A small set of sibling views exists within the same navigation location.
tabs.avoid_when: The control is acting as global app navigation.
accessibility_notes: Ensure tab semantics and keyboard navigation

platform_notes.compose: Use fixed vs scrollable tabs based on label count and width.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm indicator and label behavior with Material 3 theme.
platform_notes.web: Use tabs only for peer panes, not top-level routing.

top-app-bar.summary: Top anchored screen bar for title, context, and actions.
top-app-bar.use_when: A screen needs title hierarchy and leading/trailing actions.
top-app-bar.avoid_when: The screen is intentionally immersive and navigation-free.
accessibility_notes: Keep title and actions discoverable under edge-to-edge

platform_notes.compose: Choose small, center-aligned, medium, or large top app bar by hierarchy and scroll behavior.
platform_notes.flutter: Large and medium variants may require composition rather than a single widget.
platform_notes.web: Keep app bar semantics and spacing tokenized.

bottom-app-bar.summary: Bottom action surface, often paired with a FAB.
bottom-app-bar.use_when: Persistent bottom actions matter more than destination switching.
bottom-app-bar.avoid_when: A navigation bar is the actual primary need.
accessibility_notes: Preserve safe-area spacing and action spacing

platform_notes.compose: Reserve for action-heavy flows, not generic navigation.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm FAB notch and spacing behavior.
platform_notes.web: Use sparingly and keep insets explicit.

navigation-bar.use_when: Compact layouts need persistent top-level navigation.
navigation-bar.avoid_when: Destination count is high or width supports rail/drawer.
accessibility_notes: Keep labels visible and selected state obvious

platform_notes.compose: Default compact navigation pattern.
platform_notes.flutter: Map label visibility and selected state to Material 3.
platform_notes.web: Use only when responsive shell intentionally mirrors compact mobile behavior.

navigation-rail.use_when: Medium layouts benefit from a lateral nav spine.
navigation-rail.avoid_when: Compact width or permanent expanded drawer is a better fit.
accessibility_notes: Ensure destination order and labels remain scannable

platform_notes.compose: Default medium-width navigation pattern.
platform_notes.flutter: Use when adaptive layout reaches rail territory.
platform_notes.web: Use only for app-shell patterns with clear responsive behavior.

navigation-drawer.summary: Modal or permanent drawer for expanded navigation or grouped destinations.
navigation-drawer.use_when: Expanded widths or complex IA need grouped destinations.
navigation-drawer.avoid_when: A small set of destinations already fits a rail or bar.
accessibility_notes: Preserve focus behavior for modal drawers

platform_notes.compose: Prefer permanent drawer on expanded widths when justified.
platform_notes.flutter: Choose modal vs standard behavior explicitly.
platform_notes.web: Ensure drawer usage is tied to shell adaptation, not legacy sidebars.

fab.summary: Floating action button for the highest-priority action.
fab.use_when: One creation or forward action dominates the screen.
fab.avoid_when: Multiple actions compete or a normal button is sufficient.
accessibility_notes: Label expanded FABs or provide accessible tooltip text

platform_notes.compose: Standard, small, large, and extended variants are available.
platform_notes.flutter: Map tonal treatment and motion to Material 3 behavior.
platform_notes.web: Use only when the layout meaningfully supports it.

dialog.summary: Blocking confirmation or short focused task surface.
dialog.use_when: The user must resolve a focused decision or small task before continuing.
dialog.avoid_when: The task requires long-form interaction or full context.
accessibility_notes: Trap focus correctly
Preserve title and action semantics

platform_notes.compose: Use alert dialogs for simple confirmations and custom dialogs for bounded custom content.
platform_notes.flutter: Keep shape, tonal surface, and action hierarchy aligned.
platform_notes.web: Respect focus trapping and accessible dismissal.

bottom-sheet.summary: Supplemental sheet for supporting actions or short subflows.
bottom-sheet.use_when: Supplemental content or action groups should emerge from the bottom edge.
bottom-sheet.avoid_when: A menu or full screen is the clearer mental model.
accessibility_notes: Preserve dismissal semantics and avoid clipping content under insets

platform_notes.compose: Distinguish modal vs standard sheets clearly.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm snap and drag behavior in Material 3 mode.
platform_notes.web: Use sparingly and keep responsive behavior explicit.

snackbar.summary: Transient feedback surface with optional action.
snackbar.use_when: The user needs brief confirmation or reversible feedback.
snackbar.avoid_when: The message is critical, persistent, or needs full explanation.
accessibility_notes: Action must remain reachable and announce correctly

platform_notes.compose: Keep queueing and scaffold host behavior predictable.
platform_notes.flutter: Align action emphasis with inverse roles.
platform_notes.web: Ensure contrast and timing remain accessible.

lists.summary: Standardized list rows for repeated items.
lists.use_when: A collection of similar items needs a scan-friendly structure.
lists.avoid_when: Each item is unique enough to need a bespoke layout.
accessibility_notes: Keep row semantics and tap targets coherent

platform_notes.compose: Prefer list rows before bespoke card stacks.
platform_notes.flutter: Use list tiles or custom rows with MD3 tokens preserved.
platform_notes.web: Keep list density and separators tokenized.

menus.use_when: A compact overflow or anchored action list is needed.
menus.avoid_when: The action set is primary enough to deserve visible buttons.
accessibility_notes: Keyboard navigation and dismissal must be explicit

platform_notes.compose: Use anchored menus with clear dismissal behavior.
platform_notes.flutter: Verify Material 3 styling for popup or dropdown menu choices.
platform_notes.web: Avoid role confusion with custom popovers.

progress-indicators.use_when: Task progress or wait state must be communicated.
progress-indicators.avoid_when: The user only needs static status text.
accessibility_notes: Expose progress semantics where applicable

platform_notes.compose: Choose linear vs circular by context and available space.
platform_notes.flutter: Keep motion and semantics aligned with system expectations.
platform_notes.web: Respect reduced-motion and semantic progress roles.

date-time-pickers.use_when: Temporal input is a first-class task.
date-time-pickers.avoid_when: A simple preset or non-editable date is enough.
accessibility_notes: Preserve locale and timezone clarity where relevant

platform_notes.compose: Use only when the platform and library surface provide the needed control.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm Material 3 styling and locale behavior.
platform_notes.web: Prefer guidance over claiming full parity.

data-display-patterns.summary: Key-value, metric, and dense display patterns grounded in MD3 tokens.
data-display-patterns.use_when: Structured information must be scanned quickly.
data-display-patterns.avoid_when: A freeform illustration-first layout is the real requirement.
accessibility_notes: Maintain reading order and heading hierarchy

platform_notes.compose: Compose metrics and key-value patterns from standard surfaces and type roles.
platform_notes.flutter: Use existing MD3 surfaces rather than inventing custom dashboard atoms first.
platform_notes.web: Keep token and hierarchy usage explicit.
